ground_truth.title: For-Hire Livery Insurance Requirements | The Hartford
ground_truth.main_content: What Is Livery Insurance: Livery insurance is a type of commercial auto insurance for businesses that generate income through transporting people, like taxis, limos, ridesharing and black car services. This insurance coverage can help provide financial protection for your business, drivers and passengers if you get into an accident while on the job. For-hire livery insurance may not apply to businesses that occasionally offer transportation, such as hotel shuttle services. What Is a Livery Driver: A livery driver is a for-hire driver that generates business revenue through transporting people. The types of drivers that typically qualify as livery businesses are taxis, rideshare drivers for Uber and Lyft, limo services and black car drivers. Keep in mind that some insurance companies may not offer livery insurance for Uber and Lyft drivers. Livery Insurance Requirements May Vary by State: Insurance requirements for livery businesses vary by state. In some states, you may need more coverage than other states, which could affect your livery insurance cost. It is a good idea to check your state's local laws to find out what types of auto insurance you will need to keep your livery business safe while out on the road. Livery insurance is not like regular auto insurance. It typically has higher liability limits and covers business-related risks that regular car insurance does not protect against. Types of For-Hire Livery Insurance: When it comes to protecting your business, having the right type of coverage makes a difference when you need it most. Whether you run a transportation business or use your personal vehicle for a rideshare business, you will want to make sure you have liability protection if you get into an accident. Types of coverage include Taxi Insurance to help protect taxi drivers, their vehicles and passengers if they are involved in an accident that causes bodily injury or property damage while the taxi is in service. Black Car/Limousine Insurance is liability coverage designed to protect black car and limo services that offer pre-arranged transportation with designated pick-up and drop-off locations. Rideshare Insurance helps protect vehicles that are used for rideshare services, like Uber and Lyft. Non-Emergency Medical Transportation Insurance is available in some states for businesses that offer pre-arranged transportation services to individuals who are traveling to or from medical treatment. How Much Does Livery Insurance Cost: The cost of for-hire livery insurance often varies, but there are a number of different factors that could influence your insurance cost. Some of these factors include the state you are located in, the type of vehicle(s), your driving history, the types of coverage you choose, and your selected limits.

A livery driver is a for-hire driver that generates business revenue through transporting people.
Insurance requirements for livery businesses vary by state.
